FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class InputDispatcher

Namespace
FluentGpu.Input
Assembly
FluentGpu.Input.dll

Phase 2 (input dispatch): hit-tests the committed scene and routes pointer + keyboard. Pointer down→up over the same node fires the click handler and focuses the nearest focusable self-or-ancestor (a WinUI IsTabStop=False part never takes pointer focus; nothing focusable in the chain → focus unchanged); keyboard routes to the focused node and bubbles up ancestors (Handled stops it); Tab moves focus through focusable nodes; Enter/Space activates a focused clickable (the "one declaration, three modalities" contract). The full engine adds tunnel(Preview), gesture arena, XY-focus.

public sealed class InputDispatcher

object

ClickSlopPx 

public const float ClickSlopPx = 4

Field Value

float

DoubleClickMs 

public const uint DoubleClickMs = 500

Field Value

uint

OnCursorChanged

Raised when the resolved hover cursor changes — the host wires this to IPlatformWindow.SetCursor.

public Action<CursorId>? OnCursorChanged

Field Value

Action<CursorId>

OnHoverChanged

Set by the host: notified when a node gains/loses hover or press, so the interaction animator can ease the brush transition (kept as delegates to keep Input decoupled from the Animation assembly).

public Action<NodeHandle, bool>? OnHoverChanged

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle, bool>

OnKeyPreview

Set by the host: a global key preview run before focus routing (returns true = consumed). Lets a tree-level concern (an open overlay/flyout) intercept Escape regardless of where focus is, without stealing focus.

public Func<int, bool>? OnKeyPreview

Field Value

Func<int, bool>

OnPressChanged 

public Action<NodeHandle, bool>? OnPressChanged

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle, bool>

OnRepeatArmed

Set by the host: a RepeatButton was pressed (held) / released — drives the RepeatTicker auto-repeat.

public Action<NodeHandle>? OnRepeatArmed

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

OnRepeatPaused

Set by the host: the held pointer left / re-entered the armed repeat node — the ticker pauses off-node and resumes with a FRESH initial delay on re-entry, never an immediate re-fire (RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:530-574). Both are idempotent per move.

public Action<NodeHandle>? OnRepeatPaused

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

OnRepeatReleased 

public Action<NodeHandle>? OnRepeatReleased

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

OnRepeatResumed 

public Action<NodeHandle>? OnRepeatResumed

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

OnScrollArmed

Set by the host: arm a viewport in the ScrollAnimator after a smooth-scroll wheel (decouples Input from Animation).

public Action<NodeHandle>? OnScrollArmed

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

OnScrollHover

Set by the host: pointer is over a scrollable viewport → reveal its auto-hiding scrollbar.

public Action<NodeHandle, bool>? OnScrollHover

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle, bool>

OnScrollLeave 

public Action<NodeHandle>? OnScrollLeave

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

OnWindowActivationChanged

Raised on window activation/placement changes (WindowFocus, WindowBlur, WindowStateChanged): the host bumps the titlebar-chrome epoch signal here so a custom TitleBar re-renders (dimming / max↔restore glyph).

public Action? OnWindowActivationChanged

Field Value

Action

OnWindowBlur

Raised when the window loses activation: pressed/hover/drag state has been cleared; the host closes light-dismiss overlays here (WinUI window-deactivation dismiss).

public Action? OnWindowBlur

Field Value

Action

SmoothScroll

When true, a wheel sets the scroll TARGET and the ScrollAnimator eases the offset (momentum/inertia + auto-hiding scrollbars). When false, the offset jumps immediately (the deterministic default for tests).

public bool SmoothScroll

Field Value

bool

Properties

Clipboard

Clipboard seam for Ctrl+C over a read-only selection (WinUI TextSelectionManager::CopySelectionToClipboard, TextSelectionManager.cpp:30-41). Set by the host; null = copy is a no-op.

public IClipboard? Clipboard { get; set; }

Property Value

IClipboard

Drag

The drag-reorder gesture engine (E5-L1): armed by a press on a CanDrag chain, promoted past the 4px drag box, owning the pointer until release/Escape. Constructed with the dispatcher so every host gets item-drag without wiring; the host hooks OnSettle for the FLIP drop-glide.

public DragController Drag { get; }

Property Value

DragController

DragDrop

The typed drag-drop context (E5-L2, the Flutter Draggable/DragTarget + rbd model): a promoted L1 drag whose chain carries a BoxEl.Draggable opens THE DragSession here; per move the nearest accepting BoxEl.DropTarget under the pointer gets Enter/Over/Leave, release over it gets OnDrop, and the engine edge auto-scroll arms when the pointer drags near an overflowing viewport's edge (host-ticked).

public DragDropContext DragDrop { get; }

Property Value

DragDropContext

Focused 

public NodeHandle Focused { get; }

Property Value

NodeHandle

Fonts

Text seam for the read-only selection/hyperlink gestures (point↔index hit-testing + selection rects — the SAME editor queries EditableText drives). Null falls back to Default (set by the font-system constructors), so hosts that predate this seam still get selection without wiring.

public IFontSystem? Fonts { get; set; }

Property Value

IFontSystem

RequestRerender

Set by the host: a virtual list crossing an item boundary on scroll requests the next render.

public Action RequestRerender { get; set; }

Property Value

Action

FirstFocusableIn(NodeHandle)

First focusable within root's subtree (tab order) — for focus-trap entry / menus.

public NodeHandle FirstFocusableIn(NodeHandle root)

Parameters

root NodeHandle

Returns

NodeHandle

HitTest(Point2) 

public NodeHandle HitTest(Point2 p)

p Point2

Returns

NodeHandle

LastFocusableIn(NodeHandle)

Last focusable within root's subtree (tab order) — for Shift-Tab focus-trap wrap.

public NodeHandle LastFocusableIn(NodeHandle root)

Parameters

root NodeHandle

Returns

NodeHandle

MoveFocus(bool)

Move focus to the next/previous focusable node in tab order (TabIndex, then document order; cycles). Constrained to the active focus scope when one is pushed (dialog/flyout focus trap).

public void MoveFocus(bool forward)

Parameters

forward bool

MoveFocusArrow(FocusDirection)

Directional (arrow/XY) focus movement: from the focused node, pick the nearest focusable in dir (primary-axis distance dominates; cross-axis breaks ties). For roving lists/grids.

public void MoveFocusArrow(FocusDirection dir)

Parameters

dir FocusDirection

NextFocusableIn(NodeHandle, NodeHandle, bool)

Next/previous focusable within root, cycling — roving-tabindex within a list/menu/overlay.

public NodeHandle NextFocusableIn(NodeHandle root, NodeHandle current, bool forward = true)

Parameters

root NodeHandle
current NodeHandle
forward bool

Returns

NodeHandle

PopFocusScope() 

public void PopFocusScope()

PushFocusScope(NodeHandle)

Push a focus scope: Tab/Shift+Tab and arrow focus stay within root's subtree until popped.

public void PushFocusScope(NodeHandle root)

Parameters

root NodeHandle

RemoveFocusScope(NodeHandle)

Remove the focus scope for root wherever it sits in the stack (overlays can close out of stack order - popping blindly could drop another live trap).

public void RemoveFocusScope(NodeHandle root)

Parameters

root NodeHandle

SetFocus(NodeHandle, bool)

Move focus. visual = show the focus ring (keyboard/Tab); pointer focus passes false. Focus-visual transitions mark the affected nodes PaintDirty and request a frame — the ring is paint state. When focus actually MOVES, the old node's OnFocusChanged handler fires with false and the new node's with true (WinUI LostFocus → GotFocus order), AFTER all flag updates — so a GotFocus handler can read FocusVisual to distinguish keyboard focus (select-all) from pointer focus.

public void SetFocus(NodeHandle node, bool visual = false)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
visual bool
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
